questions.explanation: A variable holds data (e.g., numbers, strings, objects) so it can be referenced and changed during program execution.

options.A: To store a value that can be used later
options.B: To style HTML elements on a page
options.D: To compile TypeScript into Java bytecode

questions.question: In programming, what is a variable primarily used for?

questions.explanation: The <main> element represents the dominant content of the <body> and should be unique per page.
questions.question: Which HTML5 element is the most appropriate for the main content of a page (excluding headers, footers, and sidebars)?

questions.explanation: Padding adds internal spacing between the content box and the border; margin adds external spacing outside the border.
questions.question: In CSS3, which property is used to create space inside an element between its content and border?

questions.explanation: aria-label supplies an accessible name directly, helping screen readers identify the purpose of the control.
questions.question: Which ARIA attribute is commonly used to provide an accessible name for an element when a visible label is not available?

questions.explanation: Media queries allow you to apply different CSS rules based on viewport size, enabling responsive column changes.

options.A: Use a media query like @media (max-width: 600px) to change grid/flex settings
options.B:
Use
tags to force line breaks on small screens
options.C: Set all widths in pixels so the layout is consistent everywhere
options.D: Disable the viewport meta tag to prevent scaling

questions.question: You want a layout to switch from two columns to one column on small screens. Which CSS approach best supports Responsive Web Design?

questions.explanation: Event Listener Breakpoints can pause JavaScript execution when specific events (like click) are triggered, aiding debugging.
questions.question: In Chrome/Edge DevTools, which feature helps you identify why a click handler is not firing by pausing execution when the event occurs?

questions.explanation: Using the functional updater (prev => prev + 1) ensures correctness when state updates are batched or asynchronous.
questions.question: In React, what is the correct way to update state based on the previous state value?

questions.explanation: "allowJs": true lets TypeScript include and type-check .js files, enabling incremental adoption alongside .ts files.
questions.question: You are adding TypeScript to an existing JavaScript project and want to allow gradual migration while still type-checking. Which tsconfig.json setting best supports this?

questions.explanation: Code splitting reduces initial JS payload and main-thread work, improving metrics like LCP/INP by deferring non-critical code.

options.A: Add more console.log statements to measure timing
options.B: Code-split routes/components and lazy-load non-critical code
options.C: Move all CSS into inline styles in the HTML
options.D: Disable HTTP caching to ensure fresh assets

questions.question: A page loads slowly on mobile. DevTools shows a large JavaScript bundle and long main-thread tasks. Which change is most likely to improve real-world performance without changing features?

questions.explanation: Git is a distributed version control system used to track changes, collaborate via branches/merges, and manage releases.
questions.question: Your team needs to manage versions, review changes, and resolve merge conflicts for a web app. Which tool is most appropriate for software configuration management?
